title: Forrestia mollis
taxonomy: Forrestia mollis is classified as a genus within the family Icacinaceae. The family Icacinaceae is a small group of flowering plants. It is native to tropical regions of the world. Forrestia mollis is often found in forests and woodlands, where it grows as a shrub or small tree. The classification of Forrestia mollis is further supported by its botanical characteristics.

intro: Forrestia mollis is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is commonly known as the soft forestia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Forrestia mollis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
